docs.title: Welcome to the Hop2School Help Documentation
docs.text:
Hop2School is the smart solution for sustainable and efficient carpooling to school. Our software helps parents, students, and schools easily organize carpools, plan routes, and manage trips in real time.

With Hop2School, you not only help reduce traffic congestion and lower travel costs, but also contribute to protecting the environment. By sharing rides, we reduce CO₂ emissions, ease morning traffic around schools, and promote more eco-friendly mobility.

This documentation is designed to help you get the most out of Hop2School. Whether you're looking for a ride, organizing a carpool, or handling administrative tasks – you'll find all the important information and guidance here.

Use Hop2School and make the school commute more sustainable and cost-efficient!

docs.title: Dashboard
docs.text: The Hop2School dashboard provides a quick overview of upcoming trips, carpooling statistics, and the associated savings. Here are the key sections and their functions:

docs.title: 1. Left Side: Trip Overview
docs.text:
• Top: An interactive map displays all upcoming trips as markers, helping you keep track of your planned rides.
  • Below: A list of upcoming trips with the following features:
  • Chat Button (speech bubble icon): Direct access to the related chat to coordinate details with other passengers.
  • Cancel Button (cross icon): Allows you to quickly cancel a trip if your plans change.

docs.title: 2. Top Right: Offer and Acceptance Statistics
docs.text: A chart shows how often you offer rides and how often you accept rides from others. This helps you analyze and optimize your travel behavior.

docs.title: 3. Center: Savings Chart (in Euros)
docs.text:
This chart displays your daily savings from carpooling:

  • Saved travel costs: Calculated based on shared trips.
  • Additional earnings: Income from taking other passengers with you.

docs.title: 4. Bottom Right: Environmental Statistics
docs.text:
This section visualizes how your carpooling contributes to environmental protection: - CO₂ savings through shared rides. - Reduced individual traffic, helping decrease congestion and air pollution.

Goal

With these features, the Hop2School dashboard provides an efficient way to organize rides, save money, and protect the environment at the same time.

docs.title: Find Ride Offers
docs.text: The Find Ride Offers page is designed to help users find a suitable carpool opportunity. The page is structured as follows:

docs.title: Map
docs.text: At the center of the page is a large map. The vocational school Rodewisch is marked with a red marker, while available ride offers are displayed as green markers.

docs.title: Filter Search
docs.text: If you want to narrow down your search, you can click a button in the top-left corner. There, you’ll find a slider that allows you to adjust the radius for displayed rides. The selected radius is highlighted on the map so you can clearly see which offers fall within your desired area.

docs.title: Select an Offer
docs.text:
When you find a suitable offer, you can simply click on its marker. This opens a panel on the right side containing all important information about the offer.

At the top, you will see the title Offer.

Below that, all relevant trip details are listed.

Accept Offer: If you want to accept the offer, simply click the Accept button. You will then receive a confirmation email that the ride has been booked.

Clarify Questions: If you have questions or uncertainties about an offer, you can click the Contact button. This opens a chat window where you can communicate with the provider to determine whether the offer suits your needs.

Report Suspicious Activity: If an offer seems suspicious or you notice unusual content, you can use the Report button in the top-right corner of the marker to report it. If a provider receives too many reports, the offer will be reviewed and the provider may be suspended to ensure user safety.

docs.title: My Ride Offers
docs.text: The My Ride Offers page provides you with an overview of all the trips you have already offered. It is clearly structured so you can see all relevant information at a glance.

docs.title: Map (Top):
docs.text: At the top of the page, there is an interactive map displaying all locations where you have offered rides. Each marker represents a ride offer.

docs.title: Overview (Below):
docs.text:
• Title: In the center, you will see the title My Ride Offers.
  • Earnings (Left): To the left of the title, the total amount (in euros) you have earned by offering rides is displayed. This shows how much additional income you have generated by taking passengers with you.
  • Saved Trips (Right): On the right side, you can see how many trips have been saved by sharing rides. This helps you understand the positive impact of your carpooling on traffic and the environment.
  • Create New Ride Offer: To the right of the saved trips, there is a button that allows you to create a new ride offer. This is explained in more detail below.
  • Ride Overview (Below): Below the overview, you will find a table listing all the rides you have created, allowing you to view detailed information about each offer.

docs.title: Interactive Buttons (Next to Each Ride):
docs.text:
• Search Function: A button allows you to highlight a specific ride. The corresponding marker on the map will be shown in a different color for easy visual identification.

  • Edit: Another button allows you to edit an existing offer. This lets you make changes if your plans change or if you want to update ride details.

  • Cancel: A button to cancel an offer if you no longer wish to provide the ride.

Status-Dependent Buttons

Depending on the status of a ride (e.g., whether it has already been accepted or canceled), certain buttons may be hidden or disabled, as they are no longer needed. This helps keep the interface clean and easy to use.

docs.title: Create a Ride {#create-ride}
docs.text: The Create Ride page allows you to quickly and easily create a new carpool offer. Below is a detailed overview of the page structure and its features:

docs.title: Left Side – Form:
docs.text: On the left side, there is a form where you can enter all the important details for your ride offer. This includes information such as departure location, destination, time, and other relevant trip details.

docs.title: Error Messages:
docs.text: If you fill in a field incorrectly or leave something out, the affected field will be highlighted in red, and an error message will appear below it explaining the issue. This helps you quickly identify and fix mistakes.

docs.title: Required Fields:
docs.text: Not all fields need to be filled out, but some are marked with an asterisk (*). These are required fields for creating an offer. It is recommended to provide as much information as possible to help other users better understand where and when the ride takes place.

docs.title: Buttons:
docs.text:
• Cancel: A button to abort the process if you do not want to create the ride.

  • Create: A button to create the ride offer once all required data has been entered correctly.

docs.title: Right Side – Map:
docs.text:
On the right side, there is an interactive map that visually represents your entered data. Once you fill in the required fields, a marker will appear on the map showing the specified departure and/or destination.

Troubleshooting

If the marker appears far from the intended location, please check whether the address information was entered correctly. Typos or incomplete details can sometimes lead to incorrect placement. If you notice any inconsistencies, correct the address data and verify that the marker is now positioned correctly.

docs.title: Email:
docs.text: For many actions, you will receive emails to confirm important steps or to inform you about changes and cancellations. These emails keep you up to date and ensure smooth communication.

docs.title: Chats:
docs.text: The chat feature acts as a management system that displays all your previous conversations. This allows you to access information from past chats at any time. If you no longer want to see a chat, you can click the X icon to remove it. If you do not want to receive notifications for a specific chat, you can click the microphone icon button to mute the conversation.

docs.title: Language:
docs.text: In the top navigation bar, you can change the language of the website. This translates the page content into the selected language, making it accessible to more users.

docs.title: Dark/Light Mode:
docs.text: In the top navigation bar, there is an icon that allows you to switch between dark mode and light mode. This lets you choose the display mode that best suits your preferences and environment.
